title: achneria aurea
intro: Achneria aurea, commonly known as the "Golden Waxy Cap" or "Golden Chanterelle", is a species of fungus belonging to the family Hymenogastraceae. This plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties. The therapeutic actions of Achneria aurea include its use in treating infections and promoting wound healing, which is attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. While research on this plant is limited, its bioactive constituents are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties and potential health benefits.
parts: Achneria aurea has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, roots, and stems, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for their various health benefits. The leaves of Achneria aurea are rich in flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers of the plant contain sesquiterpenes, like β-sitosterol and stigmasterol, which have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of Achneria aurea contain alkaloids, including berberine and palmitic acid, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Achneria aurea can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems due to its high content of allergenic compounds. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of achneria aurea extracts can also lead to stomach issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In severe cases, improper use of achneria aurea may cause cardiovascular complications, such as rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and arrhythmias. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using achneria aurea for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
